 {"id":"bd-1csc.5","title":"Drag-and-Drop Demo","description":"Demo showcasing drag-and-drop features.

## Requirements
- Sortable list demo
- Cross-container drag
- Keyboard drag demo
- Various payload types

## Implementation
- DragDropDemo screen in showcase
- Multiple drag scenarios
- Accessibility instructions panel

## Success Criteria
- Both mouse and keyboard drag work
- Clear visual feedback
- No dropped events

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Drag-and-Drop Demo
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

Demo includes three modes: (1) Sortable List - reorder items with u/d keys, (2) Cross-Container - transfer items between lists with Enter, (3) Keyboard Drag - full keyboard-accessible drag using Space/Enter to pick up, arrows to navigate, Enter to drop. 9 unit tests cover initial state, mode navigation, selection, item movement, transfer, and rendering. Integrated into app.rs with ScreenId::DragDrop.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:38:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1csc.6","title":"Drag-and-Drop E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of drag-and-drop functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Mouse Drag (dnd_mouse_e2e.rs)
- Drag initiation threshold
- Drag preview follows cursor
- Drop zone highlighting
- Drop acceptance/rejection

### 2. Keyboard Drag (dnd_keyboard_e2e.rs)
- Pick up with Space/Enter
- Navigate targets with arrows
- Drop with Space/Enter
- Cancel with Escape

### 3. Cross-Container (dnd_cross_e2e.rs)
- Drag between lists
- Type compatibility checking
- Drop position (before/after/inside)
- Drag into nested containers

### 4. Sortable Lists (dnd_sortable_e2e.rs)
- Reorder within list
- Visual placeholder
- Auto-scroll during drag
- Drag handle mode

### 5. Edge Cases (dnd_edge_e2e.rs)
- Drag cancel mid-flight
- Drop on disabled target
- Nested drop targets
- Long distance drag scroll

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Drag lifecycle (start, move, end, cancel)
- DEBUG: Hit testing calculations
- TRACE: Position updates per frame

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/drag_drop/

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Drag-and-Drop E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

+{"id":"bd-1dhk1","title":"Fix duplicate MouseCapture / set_mouse_capture in ftui-runtime","description":"Current ftui-runtime does not compile: duplicate enum variants and duplicate methods around mouse capture. Downstream builds (e.g. mcp_agent_mail_rust cargo check --all-targets) fail with E0428 (MouseCapture defined multiple times), E0592 (set_mouse_capture defined multiple times), plus unreachable_patterns + non-exhaustive match errors.

Fix plan:
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/simulator.rs: remove the duplicated CmdRecord::MouseCapture(bool) variant; update derives/matches to be exhaustive.
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs: remove duplicate Program::set_mouse_capture; remove duplicate Cmd::SetMouseCapture match arms (causing unreachable patterns).

Acceptance:
- cargo check -p ftui-runtime --all-targets passes.
- cargo clippy -p ftui-runtime --all-targets -- -D warnings passes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:38:10.482252509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:42:59.733383820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:42:59.733365065Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","ftui-runtime"],"comments":[{"id":822,"issue_id":"bd-1dhk1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Investigated — HEAD (f4c16e42) compiles cleanly with 'cargo check -p ftui-runtime --all-targets'. The duplicate MouseCapture/SetMouseCapture variants were injected by PostToolUse hooks, not in committed code. This is a hooks artifact, not a real bug in the codebase. Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:42:59Z"}]}

Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. UBS: ran ✗ Directory too large: 8444MB exceeds limit of 1000MB
UBS copies the scan target to /tmp before analysis, which can exhaust disk space.
This safety check prevents accidental scans of large directories like $HOME.

Options:
  • Scan a smaller subdirectory instead
  • Increase the limit: UBS_MAX_DIR_SIZE_MB=5000 ubs ...
  • Disable the check: UBS_MAX_DIR_SIZE_MB=0 ubs ... (ignored demo files via .ubsignore); override  reported no changed files to scan.","created_at":"2026-02-05T02:47:58Z"},{"id":685,"issue_id":"bd-1e3w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction: UBS note had shell backticks.  {"id":"bd-1eco","title":"Text Effects: Effect Sequencer / Timeline","description":"## Background
Complex animations require manual state management. A sequencer enables declarative animation timelines that automatically transition between states.

## Implementation

Add EffectSequence struct:
```rust
pub struct EffectSequence {
    steps: Vec<SequenceStep>,
    current_step: usize,
    step_progress: f64,
    loop_mode: LoopMode,
    global_easing: Easing,
    state: SequenceState,
}

pub struct SequenceStep {
    effect: TextEffect,
    duration_secs: f64,
    easing: Option<Easing>,  // Override global easing
}

pub enum LoopMode {
    Once,           // Play once, stop at end
    Loop,           // Restart from beginning
    PingPong,       // Reverse direction at ends
    LoopCount(u32), // Loop N times then stop
}

pub enum SequenceState {
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Completed,
}

/// Events emitted by sequence (instead of callbacks to avoid lifetime issues)
pub enum SequenceEvent {
    StepStarted { step_idx: usize },
    StepCompleted { step_idx: usize },
    SequenceCompleted,
    SequenceLooped { loop_count: u32 },
}
```

Builder API:
```rust
let seq = EffectSequence::builder()
    .step(TextEffect::FadeIn { progress: 0.0 }, 0.5)
    .step(TextEffect::Pulse { speed: 2.0, min_alpha: 0.5 }, 2.0)
    .step(TextEffect::FadeOut { progress: 0.0 }, 0.5)
    .loop_mode(LoopMode::Loop)
    .easing(Easing::EaseInOut)
    .build();
```

tick() returns Option<SequenceEvent> instead of using callbacks:
```rust
impl EffectSequence {
    pub fn tick(&mut self, delta_secs: f64) -> Option<SequenceEvent> { ... }
    pub fn current_effect(&self) -> &TextEffect { ... }
    pub fn progress(&self) -> f64 { ... }  // 0.0-1.0 overall
    pub fn is_complete(&self) -> bool { ... }
    pub fn pause(&mut self) { ... }
    pub fn resume(&mut self) { ... }
    pub fn reset(&mut self) { ... }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_sequence_single_step: One step plays through
- test_sequence_multi_step: Steps transition at duration boundaries
- test_sequence_loop: Returns to step 0 after last step
- test_sequence_pingpong: Plays backward after forward
- test_sequence_loop_count: Stops after N iterations
- test_sequence_once_completes: SequenceCompleted event fires
- test_sequence_step_easing: Per-step easing overrides global
- test_sequence_event_step_started: Event fires at step boundary
- test_sequence_pause_resume: Progress freezes when paused
- test_sequence_reset: Returns to step 0, progress 0
- test_sequence_empty: Empty sequence is_complete immediately

## Integration
- StyledText::sequence(EffectSequence) applies current effect
- TransitionState can wrap a sequence for overlay animations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Effect Sequencer / Timeline
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-1emsq","title":"Cell-Mode Shape Rendering (all 8 NodeShape variants)","description":"## Goal
Implement rendering for all 8 NodeShape variants using Unicode line-drawing characters in the existing cell-based renderer. Currently only Rect is rendered — 7 shapes are silently drawn as rectangles.

## Why Critical
- The parser correctly identifies shapes from Mermaid syntax ({decision}=Diamond, ((event))=Circle, etc.)
- The IR carries NodeShape through the pipeline
- But mermaid_render.rs ignores the shape and always draws rectangles
- Without shape differentiation, diagrams lose their primary visual communication tool

## Shape Implementations (Unicode cell-mode)
- Rect: ┌─┐/└─┘ (existing, works)
- Rounded: ╭─╮/╰─╯ (round corners)
- Stadium: ╭══╮/╰══╯ with rounded ends, wider padding
- Subroutine: ║──║ double vertical borders (draw inner rect + outer vlines)
- Diamond: ╱╲/╲╱ using slash/backslash chars, requires extra width allocation
- Hexagon: ╱──╲/╲──╱ using diagonals, or Unicode ⬡ approximation
- Circle: ╭──╮/│  │/╰──╯ with aspect-ratio-compensated width (cells are ~2:1 tall:wide)
- Asymmetric: ┌──╮/└──╯▷ flag shape with pointed right side

## Width/Height Adjustments
- Diamond needs ~2× width compared to Rect for same label (diagonal borders eat interior space)
- Circle needs balanced width:height ratio — in terminal cells (roughly 2:1 aspect), a circle looks circular when width ≈ 2×height
- Layout engine may need per-shape size hints (currently uses uniform node_width/node_height)

## Files to Modify
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs: render_node dispatch on shape
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs: per-shape size in LayoutGraph (if needed)

## Tests
- Snapshot test: each shape at 80×24 with a label
- Snapshot test: mixed-shape flowchart showing visual distinction
- Unit test: border characters correct for each shape type","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:28:01.100784226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:09:44.494029975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T15:57:57.280160215Z","close_reason":"Shape rendering for all 8 NodeShape variants fully implemented + tested. Added per-shape layout sizing (Diamond 2x width/1.5x height, Circle 2x width, Stadium/Asymmetric 1.3x width, etc). Fixed diamond apex test bug. 429 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","rendering","shapes"],"comments":[{"id":742,"issue_id":"bd-1emsq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented cell-mode shape rendering for all 8 NodeShape variants (Rect, Rounded, Circle, Stadium, Subroutine, Diamond, Hexagon, Asymmetric) in mermaid_render.rs. Each shape has distinctive Unicode/ASCII glyphs and returns proper label insets. Added 14 shape-specific tests (all passing). Updated both render_nodes and render_nodes_with_plan to use shape-aware rendering. Clippy clean, fmt clean, 420 total tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:09:44Z"}]}

Note: parameterized generator (bd-3oaig.5.10) is not yet done, so generator determinism tests will be deferred until that lands.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:24:19Z"},{"id":764,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.16","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IndigoCove: Added 78 new tests (113 total, up from 35). Complete coverage of:
- Exhaustive keymap mapping (every key→action, 8 tests covering all 35+ bindings plus unbound keys)
- Full tier/glyph/render/wrap mode cycling (with epoch cascade verification)
- Palette forward/reverse cycling
- Zoom in/out with min/max clamping
- Sample navigation with analysis epoch cascade and node deselection
- Node selection/deselection/saturation
- Search mode enter/exit
- All 5 panel toggles
- CollapsePanels context-dependent behavior (inspect/search/normal)
- Epoch cascade correctness (analysis→layout→render)
- Sample registry (unique names, diagram type coverage, boundary indices)
- Config generation for all layout modes
- Layout spacing for all modes
- Cache invalidation: viewport resize, sample change, zoom change, epoch bumps
- Cache hit when only pan changes (pan doesn't invalidate)
- Viewport override edge cases (decrease from none, height increase, reset noop, target_viewport_size with/without override)
- Layout region edge cases (tiny terminal, below min_width, full width)
- Default state field verification
- All actions log to status log","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:31:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3oaig.19","title":"Comparison view integration (A/B layout modes)","description":"Goal:
- Integrate a side-by-side comparison mode for different layout settings so users can see "optimal" choices in context.

Deliverables:
- Toggle to show A/B views (e.g., default vs optimal, compact vs spacious).
- UI labeling + metrics for each side.
- Independent cache keys per side to avoid cross-contamination.

Acceptance:
- Comparison mode is optional and disabled by default.
- Works at 120x40+ with clear labels and consistent scaling.
- Snapshot test covers comparison layout for one complex sample.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:42.924605176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:05.216699173Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.19","depends_on_id":"bd-12sjj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:48.084941337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:42.924605176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:53.862314891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.19","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:57.296897412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.2","title":"Interaction model + keymap spec for mega screen","description":"Goal:
- Define the full interaction model: keybindings, modes, and on-screen help.

Deliverables:
- Keymap table covering: sample selection, complexity knobs, layout mode, sizing, zoom/pan, debug overlays, auto-fit, perf sweep, and reset shortcuts.
- Mode semantics (e.g., inspect vs auto-run) and conflict resolution rules.
- Accessibility notes: alternative bindings for arrow keys vs vim keys and a no-chord path.

Considerations:
- Keep bindings consistent with existing showcase conventions.
- Provide both single-step changes and auto-animate modes.
- Ensure help overlay uses the same source-of-truth as the keymap.

Acceptance:
- Keymap documented in-code and drives help overlay rendering.
- Every interactive knob has a visible state indicator.
- Unit test validates that key bindings map to unique actions (no silent collisions).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:28.992591041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:47:50.701076760Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:46:44.579229830Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:28.992591041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:12.664811873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":749,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented interaction model + keymap spec in mermaid.rs. Added ShowcaseMode enum (Normal/Inspect/Search), KeyCategory enum (7 categories), KeymapEntry struct, and SHOWCASE_KEYMAP const with 33 bindings covering all modes. Added keymap_for_mode() and keymap_by_category() helpers for help overlay rendering. Covers: sample nav, render config, viewport/zoom, node inspection (Tab/arrows), search (/n/N), panels, and theme cycling (p/P). 7 new tests, 457 total passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:46:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.20","title":"Structured telemetry log schema + validator","description":"Goal:
- Define and enforce a structured JSONL log schema for mega screen recompute events.

Deliverables:
- JSON schema or Rust struct for log records (timestamp, sample id, params, timings, node/edge counts, cache stats, layout quality scores).
- Validator helper used by unit tests and E2E scripts.
- Sample log fixtures for debugging.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests validate log emission fields and types.
- E2E scripts validate schema on recorded logs and fail with clear error messages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:15.401036378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:14:45.737242990Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:14:45.737198115Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","mermaid","showcase","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.20","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:15.401036378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":785,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.20","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will formalize the JSONL telemetry schema with a Rust struct, add a validator helper for unit tests and E2E scripts, and create sample log fixtures.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:07:45Z"},{"id":786,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.20","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Implementation complete. Changes:

1. Updated MEGA_TELEMETRY_REQUIRED_FIELDS to include ALL actually-emitted fields (timestamp, seed, screen_mode, styles_enabled, comparison_enabled, comparison_layout_mode, render_cols, render_rows, pan_x, pan_y, analysis/layout/render epochs, cache_hit).

2. Updated MEGA_TELEMETRY_NULLABLE_FIELDS to include all nullable fields from the emitter (run_id, node_count, edge_count, error_count, layout_iterations, layout_iterations_max, layout_budget_exceeded_layout, layout_crossings, layout_ranks, layout_max_rank_width, layout_total_bends, layout_position_variance).

3. Enhanced validate_mega_telemetry_line() with:
   - Boolean type validation (MEGA_TELEMETRY_BOOL_FIELDS)
   - Non-negative numeric validation (MEGA_TELEMETRY_NONNEG_FIELDS)
   - Timing field non-negative checks
   - String field type validation

4. Fixed run_id emission to always include field (null when absent).

5. Added 2 sample JSONL fixtures (SAMPLE_JSONL_FULL, SAMPLE_JSONL_CACHE_HIT).

6. Added 16 new validator tests:
   - validate_sample_fixture_full/cache_hit
   - validate_rejects_invalid_json/non_object/wrong_schema_version/wrong_event_type
   - validate_rejects_missing_required_field/nullable_field
   - validate_rejects_negative_zoom/boolean_as_string/negative_timing
   - validate_multi_line_jsonl/reports_bad_line
   - validate_emitted_line_with_formal_validator/without_run_id
   - sample_fixture_field_count

All 1368 demo-showcase tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:14:40Z"}]}
